name: fiftyone-develop-plugin
description: Develop custom FiftyOne plugins (operators and panels) from scratch. Use when user wants to create a new plugin, extend FiftyOne with custom operators, build interactive panels, or integrate external APIs into FiftyOne. Guides through requirements, design, coding, testing, and iteration.
capabilities: Guide developers through plugin requirements gathering and planning
Generate Python operator code for data processing tasks
Create Python panel interfaces for interactive visualization
Build JavaScript React panels for rich user experiences
Define plugin manifests with operators, panels, and secrets configuration
Test and iterate plugin implementations in local FiftyOne environments
author: AdonaiVera
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing markdown files that teach users how to develop FiftyOne plugins. All 279 static findings are false positives - they flag code examples within documentation (e.g., shell commands shown in tutorials), not actual executable code. The skill provides no network, filesystem, or code execution capabilities.
valueStatement: Users struggle to extend the FiftyOne computer vision platform with custom functionality. This skill provides step-by-step guidance for building operators and panels that integrate seamlessly with the FiftyOne App.
userTitle: Create custom FiftyOne plugins for data visualization
limitations: Does not install or modify FiftyOne plugins on user systems
Does not execute code or interact with FiftyOne datasets directly
Does not provide pre-built plugins - only teaches how to create them
Requires FiftyOne installation by the user for practical development

useCases.title: Build custom data processors
useCases.description: Create operators that transform datasets, run ML models, or export data in specialized formats

useCases.title: Extend visualization capabilities
useCases.description: Build panels that display custom metrics, interactive charts, or domain-specific visualizations

useCases.title: Integrate external APIs
useCases.description: Connect FiftyOne to external services for annotation, analysis, or data enrichment

promptTemplates.title: New plugin from scratch
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to create a new plugin
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create a new FiftyOne plugin. I want to build a [operator/panel] that [describe functionality]. Walk me through the requirements gathering, design, and implementation steps.

promptTemplates.title: Python operator guide
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create a Python operator in FiftyOne that [describe task]. Include the operator config, resolve_input, execute methods, and registration function.

promptTemplates.title: JavaScript panel template
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me build a JavaScript panel for FiftyOne using React. I need a panel that [describe UI functionality]. Show the component registration and state management.

promptTemplates.title: Plugin troubleshooting
promptTemplates.prompt: My FiftyOne plugin is not [appearing/working]. The plugin structure follows [describe structure]. Help me troubleshoot and fix the issue.

bestPractices: Always gather requirements before coding - clarify inputs, outputs, and external dependencies
Search existing plugins for patterns before implementing new functionality
Test plugins locally with small datasets before deploying to production
Follow naming conventions: @org/plugin-name for plugins, snake_case for operators and panels
antiPatterns: Skipping requirements gathering and assuming what the plugin should do
Not testing plugins in the FiftyOne App before considering them complete
Hardcoding secrets in source code instead of using the secrets configuration
Modifying files directly in site-packages instead of using the plugins directory

faq.question: What versions of FiftyOne are supported?
faq.answer: Plugins require FiftyOne 0.22 or later. Specify the minimum version in fiftyone.yml under the fiftyone.version field.

faq.question: What is the difference between operators and panels?
faq.answer: Operators perform actions like data processing and exports. Panels display interactive UIs alongside the sample grid in the FiftyOne App.

faq.question: Can I use external Python packages in my plugin?
faq.answer: Yes. List dependencies in requirements.txt. For JavaScript panels, define dependencies in package.json.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using community plugins?
faq.answer: This skill only provides documentation. When installing plugins, review code and only install from trusted sources.

faq.question: Why is my plugin not appearing in the App?
faq.answer: Check that fiftyone.yml exists in the plugin root, verify location is ~/.fiftyone/plugins/, and restart the App after installation.

faq.question: How does this compare to FiftyOne Teams plugins?
faq.answer: This skill covers standard plugin development. FiftyOne Teams adds enterprise features like remote execution and team sharing.
